explanation: This command uses rundll32.exe with a non-ADExplorer executable and avoids the 'snapshot' flag, bypassing the rule's detection of ADExplorer with the '-snapshot' parameter.
Uses /snapshot (forward slash) instead of -snapshot, and saves to \Temp\ which is not in the suspicious paths list (rule checks for \Windows\Temp\ specifically).
Renames the executable to bypass the Image|endswith detection for ADE xp.exe, ADExplorer.exe, etc. Saves to \Logs\ which is not in the suspicious paths list.
Copies the executable to System32 with a different name (sysinfo.exe), bypassing the executable name detection. Saves to \PerfLogs\ which is not in the suspicious paths list.
Uses lowercase 'adexplorer.exe' (may bypass case-sensitive matching) and saves to \Windows\Debug\ which is not in the suspicious paths list.
Uses environment variable $env:TEMP which resolves to a path not explicitly listed in the rule's suspicious paths (rule checks for literal \AppData\).

The Sigma rule requires all three selection blocks to match ('condition: all of selection_*'). By saving the snapshot to a directory that is not explicitly listed in 'selection_paths' (such as C:\PerfLogs\ instead of \Windows\Temp\ or \Users\Public\), the rule is successfully bypassed.

Similarly, C:\ProgramData\ is a common world-writable directory that attackers use, but it is missing from the rule's 'selection_paths' string list. Using this path fulfills the attacker's goal while evading this specific detection logic.
